title: pleopeltis irioides
intro: Pleopeltis irioides, also known as the Resurrection Fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to tropical America that can survive long periods of drought by curling up and becoming dormant. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The medicinal properties of Pleopeltis irioides have been linked to several health benefits, including the reduction of pain and inflammation, as well as the improvement of respiratory and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and made into tea, capsules, or tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
